rawText:
Imagine a big explosion as you climb through 3,000 ft. Imagine a plane full of smoke. Imagine an engine going clack, clack, clack. It sounds scary.

Well, I had a unique seat that day. I was sitting in 1D. I was the only one who could talk to the flight attendants. So I looked at them right away, and they said, "No problem. We probably hit some birds." The pilot had already turned the plane around, and we weren't that far. You could see Manhattan.

Two minutes later, three things happened at the same time. The pilot lines up the plane with the Hudson River. That's usually not the route. He turns off the engines. Now, imagine being in a plane with no sound. And then he says three words. The most unemotional three words I've ever heard. He says, "Brace for impact."

I didn't have to talk to the flight attendant anymore. I could see in her eyes, it was terror. Life was over.

Now I want to share with you three things I learned about myself that day. I learned that it all changes in an instant. We have this bucket list, we have these things we want to do in life, and I thought about all the people I wanted to reach out to that I didn't, all the fences I wanted to mend, all the experiences I wanted to have and I never did.

As I thought about that later on, I came up with a saying, which is, "I collect bad wines." Because if the wine is ready and the person is there, I'm opening it. I no longer want to postpone anything in life. And that urgency, that purpose, has really changed my life.

The second thing I learned that day -- and this is as we clear the George Washington Bridge, which was by not a lot -- I thought about, wow, I really feel one real regret. I've lived a good life. In my own humanity and mistakes, I've tried to get better at everything I tried. But in my humanity, I also allow my ego to get in. And I regretted the time I wasted on things that did not matter with people that matter. And I thought about my relationship with my wife, with my friends, with people. And after, as I reflected on that, I decided to eliminate negative energy from my life.

It's not perfect, but it's a lot better. I've not had a fight with my wife in two years. It feels great. I no longer try to be right; I choose to be happy.

The third thing I learned -- and this is as your mental clock starts going, "15, 14, 13." You can see the water coming. I'm saying, "Please blow up." I don't want this thing to break in 20 pieces like you've seen in those documentaries. And as we're coming down, I had a sense of, wow, dying is not scary. It's almost like we've been preparing for it our whole lives. But it was very sad. I didn't want to go; I love my life. And that sadness really framed in one thought, which is, I only wish for one thing. I only wish I could see my kids grow up.

About a month later, I was at a performance by my daughter -- first-grader, not much artistic talent -- Yet! And I'm bawling, I'm crying, like a little kid. And it made all the sense in the world to me. I realized at that point, by connecting those two dots, that the only thing that matters in my life is being a great dad. Above all, above all, the only goal I have in life is to be a good dad.

I was given the gift of a miracle, of not dying that day. I was given another gift, which was to be able to see into the future and come back and live differently.

I challenge you guys that are flying today, imagine the same thing happens on your plane -- and please don't -- but imagine, and how would you change? What would you get done that you're waiting to get done because you think you'll be here forever? How would you change your relationships and the negative energy in them? And more than anything, are you being the best parent you can?

overview.title: 3 Things I Learned While My Plane Crashed

sentences.original: Imagine a big explosion as you climb through 3,000 ft.
sentences.translation: 3,000피트를 올라갈 때 큰 폭발을 상상해 보세요.

vocabulary.example: The explosion was heard miles away.
The plane climbed rapidly after takeoff.

expressions.usage: 항공 용어로 비행기가 특정 고도를 통과하며 상승할 때

patterns.pattern: Imagine [noun] as you [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 청중에게 특정 상황을 생생하게 상상하도록 유도하는 강연 도입 패턴
examples: Imagine silence as you enter the room.
Imagine the view as you reach the summit.

drillQuestions.question: _____ a world without war.
Imagine the fear _____ you hear the alarm.

sentences.notes: TED 강연의 강렬한 첫 문장. "Imagine"으로 시작하여 청중을 즉시 비행기 사고의 현장으로 끌어들인다. "3,000 ft"는 피트(feet) 단위로, 약 914미터이다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 명령형 도입(imperative hook) — "Imagine"으로 청중의 감각적 참여를 즉시 유도

sentences.original: Imagine a plane full of smoke.
sentences.translation: 연기로 가득 찬 비행기를 상상해 보세요.

vocabulary.example: The room was full of people.
Smoke filled the air after the fire.

expressions.usage: 공간이 연기로 가득 찬 위험한 상황을 묘사할 때

patterns.pattern: Imagine [noun] full of [noun]
patterns.explanation: 특정 공간이 무언가로 가득 찬 상황을 상상하게 하는 패턴
examples: Imagine a stadium full of cheering fans.
Imagine a sky full of stars.

drillQuestions.question: Imagine a room full _____ flowers.
The cabin was _____ of smoke.

sentences.notes: Imagine의 반복(anaphora)으로 긴장감을 점층적으로 높이는 효과를 만든다. 짧은 문장으로 청중의 상상력에 하나씩 이미지를 쌓아간다.

sentences.original: Imagine an engine going clack, clack, clack.
sentences.translation: 엔진이 딸깍, 딸깍, 딸깍 소리를 내는 것을 상상해 보세요.

grammarElements.text: an engine going clack, clack, clack
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 현재분사구

vocabulary.example: The engine failed during the flight.
The typewriter went clack clack clack.

expressions.usage: 기계가 비정상적인 소리를 내는 것을 의성어로 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: Imagine [noun] [verb]-ing [onomatopoeia]
patterns.explanation: 의성어와 현재분사를 결합하여 소리를 생생하게 전달하는 패턴
examples: Imagine a clock going tick, tick, tick.
Imagine the rain going pitter-patter on the roof.

drillQuestions.question: Imagine the door _____ bang, bang, bang.
The engine was _____ a strange noise.

sentences.notes: "clack, clack, clack"는 엔진 고장 시 나는 소리를 의성어로 표현한 것이다. 세 번 반복하여 청중이 그 소리를 실제로 "듣는" 듯한 효과를 준다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 의성어(onomatopoeia) + 삼중반복(tricolon) — 소리를 세 번 반복하여 현장감 극대화

sentences.original: It sounds scary.
sentences.translation: 무섭게 들리죠.

vocabulary.example: The movie was really scary.

expressions.usage: 상황이나 이야기가 무서움을 유발할 때의 반응

patterns.pattern: It sounds [adjective]
patterns.explanation: 감각동사 sound를 사용하여 무언가에 대한 인상이나 느낌을 표현하는 패턴
examples: It sounds exciting.
It sounds complicated.

drillQuestions.question: That _____ amazing!
It sounds _____ but it was real.

sentences.notes: 극적인 장면 묘사 후에 짧은 문장으로 마무리하여 청중과의 공감대를 형성한다. 연사가 잠시 숨을 고르는 역할도 한다.

sentences.original: Well, I had a unique seat that day.
sentences.translation: 그런데, 그날 저는 특별한 좌석에 앉았습니다.

vocabulary.example: Everyone has a unique fingerprint.

expressions.usage: 이야기의 전환이나 새로운 정보를 도입할 때 사용하는 담화 표지

patterns.pattern: Well, I had a [adjective] [noun] that day.
patterns.explanation: 개인적 경험을 이야기할 때 특별한 상황을 도입하는 스토리텔링 패턴
examples: Well, I had an amazing experience that day.
Well, I had an unusual feeling that morning.

drillQuestions.question: _____, I had a strange feeling that day.
I _____ a unique opportunity that year.

sentences.notes: "Well"은 이야기의 전환을 자연스럽게 만드는 담화 표지(discourse marker)이다. "1D"라는 좌석은 비행기 맨 앞줄로, 승무원과 직접 대화할 수 있는 위치이다.

sentences.original: The pilot had already turned the plane around, and we weren't that far.
sentences.translation: 조종사는 이미 비행기를 돌렸고, 우리는 그렇게 멀리 있지 않았습니다.

vocabulary.example: The driver turned the car around.
She had already left when I arrived.

expressions.usage: 거리가 예상보다 가까울 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] had already [past participle] ...
patterns.explanation: 과거완료로 특정 과거 시점 이전에 이미 완료된 행동을 표현하는 패턴
examples: She had already finished dinner when he called.
They had already left the building.

drillQuestions.question: The pilot _____ already turned the plane around.
We had already _____ when they arrived.

sentences.notes: 과거완료(had turned)는 "이미 그 전에 돌렸다"는 시간적 선후 관계를 명확히 한다. "turn around"은 구동사로, 물리적으로 방향을 바꾸는 것을 의미한다.

koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 우리는 그렇게 멀리 있지 않았습니다

sentences.original: The pilot lines up the plane with the Hudson River.
sentences.translation: 조종사가 비행기를 허드슨강과 일직선으로 맞춥니다.

vocabulary.example: The pilot lined up the aircraft with the runway.

expressions.usage: 항공에서 착륙 방향을 정렬할 때, 또는 일반적으로 정렬할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] lines up [object] with [target]
patterns.explanation: 대상을 특정 목표와 정렬시키는 동작을 표현하는 패턴. 여기서는 역사적 현재시제로 생동감을 더한다.
examples: The archer lines up the arrow with the target.
She lines up the car with the parking space.

drillQuestions.question: The pilot _____ up the plane with the runway.
He lined the car up _____ the garage.

sentences.notes: 과거 사건인데 현재 시제(lines up)를 사용하는 "역사적 현재(historical present)"이다. 이는 청중이 마치 그 순간에 있는 것처럼 느끼게 하는 스토리텔링 기법이다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역사적 현재(historical present) — 과거 사건을 현재 시제로 서술하여 생동감과 긴박감을 극대화

sentences.original: He turns off the engines.
sentences.translation: 그가 엔진을 끕니다.

vocabulary.example: Please turn off the lights.

expressions.usage: 기계나 장치를 정지시킬 때

patterns.pattern: He [verb]s off the [noun].
patterns.explanation: 역사적 현재로 극적인 행동을 짧게 전달하는 패턴
examples: He shuts off the power.
She switches off the computer.

drillQuestions.question: He turns _____ the engines.
She _____ off the light before leaving.

sentences.notes: 비행기 엔진을 끈다는 것은 극도로 위험한 상황을 의미한다. 이 짧은 문장은 역사적 현재시제와 함께 그 충격적인 순간을 극대화한다.

sentences.original: Now, imagine being in a plane with no sound.
sentences.translation: 자, 소리가 전혀 없는 비행기 안에 있는 것을 상상해 보세요.

grammarElements.text: being in a plane with no sound
grammarElements.label: 동명사구 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: There was not a sound in the room.

expressions.usage: 완전한 고요함을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: Imagine [verb]-ing in [place] with no [noun]
patterns.explanation: 무언가가 부재한 상황에서의 경험을 상상하게 하는 패턴
examples: Imagine living in a city with no noise.
Imagine working in an office with no windows.

drillQuestions.question: Imagine _____ in a world with no internet.
Imagine being in a forest with no _____.

sentences.notes: 비행기에서 엔진 소리가 사라진 순간의 공포를 전달한다. "with no sound"는 "without any sound"보다 더 극적이고 구어적인 표현이다.

sentences.original: And then he says three words. The most unemotional three words I've ever heard. He says, "Brace for impact."
sentences.translation: 그리고 그가 세 단어를 말합니다. 제가 들어본 것 중 가장 감정 없는 세 단어입니다. "충격에 대비하세요."

grammarElements.text: The most unemotional three words I've ever heard
grammarElements.label: 동격 (부연)

vocabulary.example: He delivered the news in an unemotional tone.
Brace yourself for the bad news.
The impact of the crash was enormous.

expressions.usage: 항공에서 비상 착륙 시 승객에게 안전 자세를 취하라는 지시
최상급 경험을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: The most [adjective] [noun] I've ever [past participle]
patterns.explanation: 최상급 + 현재완료를 결합하여 지금까지의 경험 중 가장 ~한 것을 표현하는 패턴
examples: It was the most beautiful sunset I've ever seen.
This is the most difficult exam I've ever taken.

drillQuestions.question: It was the most amazing thing I've ever _____.
The _____ unemotional words I've ever heard.

sentences.notes: "Brace for impact"은 항공 비상 시 표준 지시문이다. 연사가 "가장 무감정한 세 단어"라고 표현한 것은, 조종사의 차분한 전달과 그 내용의 충격적인 의미 사이의 극적 대비(dramatic irony)를 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: And then he says three words
The most unemotional three words I've ever heard
koreanAlignment.ko: 제가 들어본 것 중 가장 감정 없는 세 단어

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 극적 아이러니(dramatic irony) — 차분한 톤과 끔찍한 내용의 대조

sentences.original: I could see in her eyes, it was terror. Life was over.
sentences.translation: 그녀의 눈에서 볼 수 있었습니다, 공포였습니다. 삶이 끝난 것이었습니다.

vocabulary.example: A look of terror crossed her face.

expressions.usage: 상대방의 감정을 눈빛으로 파악할 때
절망적 상황에서 모든 것이 끝났다고 느낄 때

patterns.pattern: I could see in [possessive] eyes, it was [emotion].
patterns.explanation: 상대방의 눈에서 감정을 읽었음을 표현하는 스토리텔링 패턴
examples: I could see in his eyes, it was joy.
I could see in their eyes, it was disbelief.

drillQuestions.question: I could see in her _____, it was fear.
I _____ see the terror in his expression.

sentences.notes: 두 개의 매우 짧은 문장이 연속되어 충격과 절망을 간결하게 전달한다. "Life was over."는 단 세 단어로 사형 선고와 같은 무게감을 가진다.

koreanAlignment.en: I could see in her eyes

sentences.original: I learned that it all changes in an instant.
sentences.translation: 모든 것이 한순간에 변한다는 것을 배웠습니다.

grammarElements.text: that it all changes in an instant
grammarElements.label: that절 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: In an instant, everything was gone.

expressions.usage: 매우 빠르게 일어나는 변화를 강조할 때
삶의 근본적 변화를 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: I learned that [something] [verb]s in [time expression]
patterns.explanation: 경험에서 배운 교훈을 전달하는 패턴
examples: I learned that everything can change in a moment.
I learned that trust builds over time.

drillQuestions.question: I _____ that life is short.
Everything changed in an _____.

sentences.notes: 연사가 배운 첫 번째 교훈의 핵심 문장이다. "in an instant"는 "in a moment"와 비슷하지만, 더 극적이고 갑작스러운 느낌을 준다.

sentences.original: I no longer want to postpone anything in life.
sentences.translation: 저는 더 이상 삶에서 어떤 것도 미루고 싶지 않습니다.

vocabulary.example: We had to postpone the meeting.
She no longer lives here.

expressions.usage: 과거에는 했지만 이제는 하지 않겠다는 결심을 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: I no longer [verb] anything in life
patterns.explanation: 인생에서의 태도 변화를 선언하는 패턴. no longer는 부정의 변화를 나타낸다.
examples: I no longer worry about small things in life.
I no longer take anything for granted.

drillQuestions.question: I no _____ want to waste time.
She no longer wants to _____ her dreams.

sentences.notes: "I collect bad wines"이라는 비유에서 도출된 교훈이다. postpone(미루다)를 no longer와 결합하여 삶에 대한 태도 변화를 강력하게 선언한다.

sentences.original: And that urgency, that purpose, has really changed my life.
sentences.translation: 그리고 그 긴박감, 그 목적의식이 제 삶을 정말로 바꿔놓았습니다.

vocabulary.example: There is a sense of urgency about climate change.
She found her purpose in life.

expressions.usage: 인생의 근본적인 변화를 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: That [noun], that [noun], has [past participle] ...
patterns.explanation: 동격 반복으로 핵심 개념을 강조하고 리듬감을 만드는 연설 패턴
examples: That courage, that determination, has brought us here.
That love, that commitment, has kept us together.

drillQuestions.question: That urgency has really _____ my life.
That _____, that drive, made all the difference.

sentences.notes: "that urgency, that purpose"에서 that의 반복은 강조와 리듬을 동시에 만든다. 현재완료(has changed)로 과거의 경험이 현재까지 영향을 미치고 있음을 표현한다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 동격 반복(apposition) — "that urgency, that purpose"로 핵심 개념을 강조

sentences.original: And I regretted the time I wasted on things that did not matter with people that matter.
sentences.translation: 그리고 중요한 사람들과 함께하면서 중요하지 않은 일에 낭비한 시간을 후회했습니다.

grammarElements.text: on things that did not matter
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: I regret not studying harder.
Don't waste your time on trivial matters.
What matters most is your health.

expressions.usage: 무의미한 일이나 활동을 표현할 때
소중한 관계를 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: I regretted the time I wasted on [things] with [people]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 시간 낭비를 후회하는 내용을 전달하는 패턴
examples: I regretted the hours I wasted on social media.
She regretted the years she spent in the wrong career.

drillQuestions.question: I regretted the time I _____ on unimportant things.
Focus on things that _____ to you.

sentences.notes: "things that did not matter with people that matter"에서 matter의 반복이 핵심이다. "중요하지 않은 일 + 중요한 사람"이라는 아이러니한 조합이 후회의 깊이를 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: on things that did not matter

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조법(antithesis) + 반복법 — "did not matter"와 "matter"의 대조적 반복

sentences.original: I decided to eliminate negative energy from my life.
sentences.translation: 저는 삶에서 부정적인 에너지를 없애기로 결심했습니다.

vocabulary.example: We need to eliminate waste from the process.
Negative energy can affect your relationships.

expressions.usage: 의지적 결정을 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: I decided to [verb] [something] from my life
patterns.explanation: 인생에서 무언가를 제거하겠다는 결심을 표현하는 패턴
examples: I decided to remove toxic people from my life.
I decided to cut out stress from my daily routine.

drillQuestions.question: I decided _____ eliminate bad habits.
She _____ to change her lifestyle.

sentences.notes: eliminate는 remove보다 더 강하고 완전한 제거를 의미한다. 부정적 에너지를 삶에서 완전히 없애겠다는 강한 결심을 보여주는 문장이다.

sentences.original: I no longer try to be right; I choose to be happy.
sentences.translation: 저는 더 이상 옳으려고 하지 않습니다; 행복하기를 선택합니다.

vocabulary.example: You can choose to see the positive side.

expressions.phrase: I no longer try to be right
expressions.usage: 관계에서 승리보다 화합을 우선할 때
의지적으로 행복을 추구하는 태도를 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: I no longer [verb] to be [A]; I choose to be [B]
patterns.explanation: 이전의 태도를 버리고 새로운 태도를 선택했음을 대조적으로 표현하는 패턴
examples: I no longer strive to be perfect; I choose to be authentic.
I no longer try to be popular; I choose to be genuine.

drillQuestions.question: I no longer try to be right; I _____ to be happy.
She no _____ tries to impress others.

sentences.notes: 세미콜론(;)으로 연결된 두 절이 강렬한 대조를 이룬다. "be right vs. be happy"는 많은 관계에서의 핵심 갈등을 요약하는 명언급 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: I no longer try to be right
koreanAlignment.ko: 저는 더 이상 옳으려고 하지 않습니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조법(antithesis) — "be right" vs "be happy"의 날카로운 대비

sentences.original: And as we're coming down, I had a sense of, wow, dying is not scary.
sentences.translation: 그리고 우리가 내려가고 있을 때, 와, 죽는 것은 무섭지 않다는 느낌이 들었습니다.

vocabulary.example: I had a sense of peace.
He was not afraid of dying.

expressions.usage: 직관적이고 감각적인 느낌을 표현할 때
비행기가 착륙 또는 추락하는 것을 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: As we're [verb]-ing, I had a sense of [clause]
patterns.explanation: 진행 중인 상황 속에서의 내적 깨달음을 전달하는 패턴
examples: As we were driving, I had a sense of something wrong.
As she was speaking, I had a sense of truth in her words.

drillQuestions.question: As we're coming down, I _____ a sense of calm.
_____ is not as scary as we think.

sentences.notes: 죽음에 대한 의외의 깨달음을 전달하는 문장이다. "wow"라는 감탄사가 삽입되어 그 순간의 놀라움을 생생하게 전달한다. 구어체 특성이 강한 문장이다.

sentences.original: It's almost like we've been preparing for it our whole lives.
sentences.translation: 마치 평생 그것을 준비해 온 것 같았습니다.

vocabulary.example: She prepared for the exam for months.
I spent the whole day studying.

expressions.usage: 비유적 비교를 할 때
매우 긴 기간을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: It's almost like we've been [verb]-ing ... our whole lives
patterns.explanation: 평생에 걸친 무의식적 준비나 경험을 비유하는 패턴
examples: It's almost like we've been waiting for this moment our whole lives.
It's almost like they've been training for this their entire careers.

drillQuestions.question: It's almost like we've been _____ for this moment.
It's _____ like a dream come true.

sentences.notes: 죽음이 새로운 것이 아니라 삶 전체가 그 준비 과정이었다는 깊은 통찰을 담은 문장이다. 현재완료진행형으로 지속적이고 무의식적인 준비를 표현한다.

sentences.original: I only wish I could see my kids grow up.
sentences.translation: 오직 제 아이들이 자라는 것을 볼 수 있기만을 바랐습니다.

grammarElements.text: I could see my kids grow up
grammarElements.label: 가정법 절

vocabulary.example: I wish I could travel the world.
I grew up in a small town.

expressions.usage: 가장 간절한 단 하나의 소원을 표현할 때
부모로서의 가장 근본적인 소망을 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: I only wish I could [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 가정법으로 실현 불가능할 수 있는 간절한 소망을 표현하는 패턴
examples: I only wish I could turn back time.
I only wish I could be there with you.

drillQuestions.question: I only wish I _____ see my family again.
I wish I could see my kids _____ up.

sentences.notes: 강연의 가장 감성적인 순간이다. "only"는 다른 모든 소원이 사라지고 단 하나만 남았음을 강조한다. could는 가정법으로, 실현 불가능할 수 있는 소망을 표현한다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 감정적 절정(emotional climax) — 가장 단순하고 보편적인 소망으로 청중의 감정을 극대화

sentences.original: I realized at that point, by connecting those two dots, that the only thing that matters in my life is being a great dad.
sentences.translation: 그 순간, 그 두 가지를 연결함으로써, 제 인생에서 유일하게 중요한 것은 훌륭한 아빠가 되는 것이라는 걸 깨달았습니다.

grammarElements.text: that the only thing that matters in my life is being a great dad
grammarElements.label: that절 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: I realized I had made a mistake.
It took time to connect the dots.

expressions.usage: 서로 다른 경험이나 사실 사이의 연관성을 발견할 때
가장 중요한 단 하나의 가치를 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: I realized that the only thing that matters is [verb]-ing ...
patterns.explanation: 인생의 가장 중요한 가치를 깨달은 순간을 표현하는 패턴
examples: I realized that the only thing that matters is being present.
She realized that the only thing that matters is love.

drillQuestions.question: I _____ that family is the most important thing.
The only thing that _____ is being kind.

sentences.notes: "connecting those two dots"는 Steve Jobs의 유명한 "connecting the dots" 개념과 유사하다. 비행기 사고에서의 마지막 소원(아이들의 성장)과 딸의 공연에서의 감동을 연결하여 인생의 가장 중요한 가치를 발견한 순간이다.

koreanAlignment.en: that the only thing that matters in my life

sentences.original: I was given the gift of a miracle, of not dying that day.
sentences.translation: 저는 그날 죽지 않는, 기적이라는 선물을 받았습니다.

vocabulary.example: It was a miracle that everyone survived.
Life itself is a precious gift.

expressions.usage: 예상치 못한 소중한 경험이나 기회를 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: I was given the gift of [noun], of [verb]-ing ...
patterns.explanation: 수동태로 겸손하게 받은 은혜를 표현하고, 동격으로 구체적 내용을 부연하는 패턴
examples: I was given the gift of time, of spending another year with my family.
She was given the gift of talent, of being able to inspire others.

drillQuestions.question: I was _____ the gift of a second chance.
The gift of a _____ saved his life.

sentences.notes: 수동태(was given)를 사용하여 기적이 자신의 능력이 아니라 외부에서 주어진 것임을 겸손하게 표현한다. "of not dying"은 생존 자체를 선물로 재정의하는 강력한 표현이다.

sentences.original: I was given another gift, which was to be able to see into the future and come back and live differently.
sentences.translation: 또 다른 선물을 받았는데, 그것은 미래를 들여다보고 돌아와서 다르게 살 수 있는 것이었습니다.

grammarElements.text: which was to be able to see into the future and come back and live differently
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (보충 설명)

vocabulary.example: After the experience, she saw the world differently.

expressions.usage: 앞으로 일어날 일을 미리 경험하는 것을 비유적으로 표현할 때
변화된 삶의 방식을 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: I was given [noun], which was to [verb] and [verb] and [verb]
patterns.explanation: 받은 선물의 내용을 세 가지 행동으로 풀어 설명하는 패턴
examples: I was given a chance, which was to learn and grow and succeed.
She was given an opportunity, which was to travel and explore and discover.

drillQuestions.question: I was given another gift, _____ was to see the future.
He decided to live _____ after the experience.

sentences.notes: "see into the future and come back"은 비행기 추락 순간에 미래(죽음)를 미리 본 경험을 은유적으로 표현한 것이다. 세 가지 행동(see, come back, live)이 and로 연결되어 리듬감을 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: to be able to see into the future
and come back and live differently
koreanAlignment.ko: 돌아와서 다르게 살 수 있는 것

sentences.original: I challenge you guys that are flying today, imagine the same thing happens on your plane -- and please don't -- but imagine, and how would you change?
sentences.translation: 오늘 비행기를 타는 여러분에게 도전합니다, 같은 일이 여러분의 비행기에서 일어난다고 상상해 보세요 -- 제발 그러지 않기를 -- 하지만 상상해 보세요, 여러분은 어떻게 변할 건가요?

grammarElements.text: you guys that are flying today
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: imagine the same thing happens on your plane
grammarElements.label: 명령절

vocabulary.example: I challenge you to think differently.

expressions.usage: 청중에게 행동이나 성찰을 촉구할 때
심각한 내용을 유머로 완화할 때
가정법으로 변화의 가능성을 질문할 때

patterns.pattern: I challenge you to imagine ... and how would you [verb]?
patterns.explanation: 청중에게 가정적 시나리오를 상상하고 자기 성찰을 하도록 촉구하는 결론 패턴
examples: I challenge you to imagine losing everything, and how would you rebuild?
I challenge you to imagine your last day, and how would you spend it?

drillQuestions.question: I _____ you to think about your priorities.
How _____ you change your life?

sentences.notes: 강연의 마무리에서 청중에게 직접적으로 도전하는 문장이다. "and please don't"이라는 삽입 유머가 무거운 주제를 가볍게 만들어주며, 가정법 의문문으로 자기 성찰을 유도한다.

koreanAlignment.en: I challenge you guys that are flying today
imagine the same thing happens on your plane
koreanAlignment.ko: 같은 일이 여러분의 비행기에서 일어난다고 상상해 보세요

koreanAlignment.en: but imagine, and how would you change
koreanAlignment.ko: 하지만 상상해 보세요, 어떻게 변할 건가요

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 청중 참여 유도(audience engagement) + 유머 완화(comic relief) — 무거운 도전 속에 유머를 삽입

sentences.original: And more than anything, are you being the best parent you can?
sentences.translation: 그리고 무엇보다, 여러분은 될 수 있는 최고의 부모가 되고 있나요?

vocabulary.example: More than anything, I want my family to be safe.

expressions.usage: 가장 중요한 것을 강조할 때 사용하는 최상급 표현
최선을 다하고 있는지 묻거나 촉구할 때

patterns.pattern: Are you being the best [noun] you can?
patterns.explanation: 현재 최선을 다하고 있는지 자기 성찰을 촉구하는 패턴
examples: Are you being the best friend you can?
Are you being the best version of yourself?

drillQuestions.question: _____ you being the best student you can?
More than _____, be a good parent.

sentences.notes: 강연의 마지막 질문으로, 가장 보편적이고 강력한 메시지를 담고 있다. "the best parent you can"에서 "be"가 생략되어 있다(= the best parent you can be). 현재진행형(are you being)은 지금 이 순간의 행동을 묻는다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수사적 질문(rhetorical question) — 강연의 핵심 메시지를 질문 형태로 남기며 여운을 만듦
